item.dc:creator: UnlimitedTrickPony

item.title: This is the Ukrainian soldier Oleksii, callsign "Botanik." He spent 343 days on the front lines without rotation .. voluntarily.
item.description: CNN reports on the story of 37-year-old Ukrainian Ground Forces infantry officer Oleksii, callsign "Botanik." He served in the Zaporizhzhia direction for almost a year, from April 1, 2025, to March 8, 2026. The publication states that he stayed on the front voluntarily due to a shortage of personnel in his unit: "There are not enough people in my company, and about half of the soldiers are over 50 years old. Ideally, it's one month on the position, one month of recovery, but right now that is unrealistic." After 343 days, he was given a short leave. He celebrated his daughter's 10th birthday, taught her to ride a bicycle, and then returned to his unit.
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: I’m hungry…for memes!!!
item.description:
Welcome to this dump of random memes. Sometimes I make them themed, sometimes I don't. If you enjoy laughing without talking politics, getting away from the horrors of the world, or escaping reality, you’ll love following this page!
Super fierce. Slay!
Would pay money to see the aftermath
Perfect
He is a man of his word
Should’ve been a middle aged woman
Grandpa can kick rocks
Did she not feel super fat? Everyone asked me if I was having twins but it was just a big baby, but surely three babies would make one big as a barn
I’ll have mine delivered in Cartier
Are we not still doing this?
Glad nobody showed up nekkid on the bottom
I’m sure it is very relaxing and kinda boring
Kiddos who wake up at 5am is the reason we can’t stay out late
For a free shake? Heck yeah
True love
Who is this?
Who forgets an entire drawer of deliciousness?
Truth has been told here
OMG! Does it only come on Halloween?
Mint has a very fine line of delicious or toothpaste
Grossest taste ever
Maybe she’s a bad Avatar cosplayer
AI could never
Best dad joke
Creepy
The joke is the taste
Methuselah probably just ate a candle
It was a jooooooke
Seriously, it was EVERYWHERE for decades
Finishing the project means it was made with love because only love could make be reach the end
Never works
Dontcha wish your girlfriend was hot like me
Baptism by fire with kids
Come on, I spent $100 on this team and $300 gear!
Every night
Peak parenting
Children to adult sizes is tricky
Guess I just die then
Why did we think those chunks were cool?
Tis magic
Shhhh
Teach him now
Husband and kids, every day
Maybe she should just get a goldfish
Yup
Especially with kids
I don’t like to clean up flour
Happened to me this week
item.dc:creator: GigglySprinkleCupcake

item.title: This goes for everyone!!! Make sure the person you settle down with is the person at 90 you want to be arguing over the remote with... Forget everything else, life happens, just make sure you're with your best friend at the end!

item.title: Disney re-animated songs from several of their films but with the characters using ASL
item.description: And they didn't just use AI, if that was your first thought. They hired deaf actors to choreograph and act out the scenes, filmed it, and used that as a reference. Part of a special series to honor Deaf History Month

item.title: Didn't know this was a thing (des Landes region, France)
item.description:
At that time, the Landes region was a vast, flat marshland where mainly grasses and scrub grew. This landscape was inhabited primarily by shepherds. To move more easily through the vegetation and keep their feet dry in the marshland, but especially to be able to watch over their flocks of sheep over long distances, the shepherds used stilts. The earliest evidence of the use of stilts dates back to the early 18th century.
Inhabitants of the Landes, an idealized depiction of the Landes de Gascogne. (Jean-Louis Gintrac (1808–1886), Inhabitants of the Landes, Bordeaux Museum of Fine Arts; first half of the 19th century; oil on canvas; 32 x 46 cm).
In La Teste, Landes. Shepherds from the Landes region standing on stilts, by Théodore Caruelle d'Aligny
item.dc:creator: UnlimitedTrickPony

item.title: 41 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 248
item.description:
Arizona sunsets from https://www.instagram.com/p/DGUCB9qxpTW/
Ernesto's Cat Sanctuary in Syria, founded by Mohammad Alaa Aljaleel, provides shelter, food, and medical care for abandoned cats amidst the ongoing conflict. It serves as a refuge for these animals and has become a symbol of hope and compassion in a war-torn area.
item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: 1963 Chrysler Imperial, which doesn't use light bulbs.
item.description: Back in the early ’60s, Chrysler did something wild with their dashboards: they used electroluminescent (EL) lighting instead of bulbs. Instead of shining light onto the gauges, the gauges themselves glowed. Inside the panel is a thin layer of phosphor sandwiched between conductive layers. When you apply high-voltage AC (around 100–200 volts at high frequency), it excites the phosphor and it emits that signature soft green-blue light. No hotspots, no shadows. Just an even, cool glow. The catch? Cars run on 12V DC, so Chrysler had to add a separate inverter box under the dash to step that up into high-voltage AC. It worked beautifully when new, but the electronics were expensive and not super reliable, so by the late ’60s they quietly went back to traditional bulbs. Still one of the coolest pieces of automotive tech from that era.
item.dc:creator: CitizenK9

item.title: A Fine Assortment of Stolen Memes, Vol. 17
item.description:
And then not having any energy to do anything about it, that's fun
I like to think that my whimsy helps keep me employed.
I was eating a bagel and lox every morning for nearly two months. Delicious, but then I started getting tummy aches.
I used to live off those things. Now I look at one funny and I start feeling irritable.
item.dc:creator: Vercury

item.title: Your tax dollars go to these people.
item.description: If you live in the US, UK, Germany, your tax dollars are involved in direct military aid to Israel. If you live in the EU, you still give them funds indirectly through European Neighbourhood Instrument (ENI) and Horizon Europe.
